findings.recommendation: Install the server as a pinned dependency and invoke the local binary, or vendor it. If a runner is unavoidable, pin an exact version (see unpinned_source).

tools.name: add_variable_mode
tools.description: Add a new mode to an existing variable collection (e.g. Light/Dark, Desktop/Mobile). IMPORTANT — Figma free plan only allows 1 mode per collection; calling this tool on a free-plan account will return the error 'Limited to 1 modes only'. If that error occurs, stop retrying and switch to the name-prefix workaround: keep the single default mode and create variables prefixed by mode, e.g. 'light/color-bg' and 'dark/color-bg' in the same collection. Tell the user that native multi-mode variables require a paid Figma plan (Professional or above).

tools.name: apply_style_to_node
tools.description: Apply an existing local style (paint, text, effect, or grid) to a node, linking the node to that style.

tools.name: batch_rename_nodes
tools.description: Rename multiple nodes using find/replace, regex substitution, or prefix/suffix addition.

tools.name: bind_variable_to_node
tools.description: Bind a local variable to a node property so the property is driven by the variable's value. COLOR variables: use fillColor or strokeColor. BOOLEAN variables: use visible. FLOAT variables: use opacity, rotation, width, height, cornerRadius, topLeftRadius, topRightRadius, bottomLeftRadius, bottomRightRadius, strokeWeight, itemSpacing, paddingTop, paddingRight, paddingBottom, paddingLeft.

tools.name: clone_node
tools.description: Clone an existing node, optionally repositioning it or placing it in a new parent.

tools.name: create_component
tools.description: Convert an existing FRAME node into a reusable COMPONENT. The frame is replaced in place by the new component.

tools.name: create_effect_style
tools.description: Create a new local effect style (drop shadow, inner shadow, or blur).

tools.name: create_ellipse
tools.description: Create a new ellipse (circle/oval) on the current page or inside a parent node.

tools.name: create_frame
tools.description: Create a new frame on the current page or inside a parent node.

tools.name: create_grid_style
tools.description: Create a new local layout grid style.

tools.name: create_paint_style
tools.description: Create a new local paint style with a solid fill color.

tools.name: create_rectangle
tools.description: Create a new rectangle on the current page or inside a parent node.

tools.name: create_section
tools.description: Create a Figma Section node on the current page. Sections are the modern way to organize frames and groups on a page.

tools.name: create_text
tools.description: Create a new text node on the current page or inside a parent node. The font is loaded automatically before insertion. Returns the created node ID and bounds. Use set_text to update the content of an existing text node.

tools.name: create_text_style
tools.description: Create a new local text style (typography preset). Returns the new style's ID. Apply it to nodes with apply_style_to_node. Use get_styles to list existing text styles.

tools.name: create_variable
tools.description: Create a new variable (design token) inside an existing collection. Returns the new variable's ID. Use get_variable_defs to find collection IDs, set_variable_value to set values per mode, and bind_variable_to_node to apply the variable to a node property.

tools.name: create_variable_collection
tools.description: Create a new local variable collection with an optional initial mode name. NOTE — Figma free plan limits each collection to 1 mode. If you need Light/Dark (or any multi-mode) theming and the user is on the free plan, do NOT try to call add_variable_mode; instead use the name-prefix workaround: create all variables in a single collection and prefix each variable name with its mode, e.g. 'light/color-bg' and 'dark/color-bg'. Inform the user of this limitation.

tools.name: delete_nodes
tools.description: Delete one or more nodes. This cannot be undone via MCP — use with care.

tools.name: get_viewport
tools.description: Get the current Figma viewport: scroll center, zoom level, and visible bounds.

tools.name: group_nodes
tools.description: Group two or more nodes into a GROUP. All nodes must share the same parent.

tools.name: import_image
tools.description: Import a base64-encoded image into Figma as a rectangle with an image fill. Use get_screenshot to capture images or provide your own base64 PNG/JPG.

tools.name: lock_nodes
tools.description: Lock one or more nodes to prevent accidental edits in Figma.

tools.name: move_nodes
tools.description: Move one or more nodes to an absolute canvas position. The same x/y is applied to every node independently (not a relative offset from current position).

tools.name: navigate_to_page
tools.description: Switch the active Figma page. Provide either pageId or pageName.

tools.name: remove_reactions
tools.description: Remove prototype reactions from a node. Omit indices to remove all reactions. Provide a zero-based indices array to remove specific reactions (use get_reactions first to see current indices).

tools.name: rename_node
tools.description: Rename a single node by ID. Returns the updated node with its new name. Use batch_rename_nodes to rename multiple nodes at once or to apply find/replace patterns across many nodes.

tools.name: rename_page
tools.description: Rename an existing page in the Figma document.

tools.name: reorder_nodes
tools.description: Change the z-order (layer stack position) of one or more nodes.

tools.name: reparent_nodes
tools.description: Move one or more nodes to a different parent frame, group, or section.

tools.name: resize_nodes
tools.description: Resize one or more nodes. The same width/height is applied to every node in the list independently. Provide width, height, or both.

tools.name: rotate_nodes
tools.description: Rotate one or more nodes to an absolute angle in degrees.

tools.name: save_screenshots
tools.description: Export screenshots for multiple nodes and write them to the local filesystem. Returns file metadata (path, size, dimensions) — no base64 in the response. Use get_screenshot instead when you need the image data in memory.

tools.name: scan_nodes_by_types
tools.description: Find all nodes of specific types in a subtree, regardless of name. Use search_nodes instead when you need to filter by name.

tools.name: scan_text_nodes
tools.description: Scan all TEXT nodes in a subtree and return their content. Shorthand for scan_nodes_by_types with ['TEXT'] — use when you only need text copy from a component or frame.

tools.name: search_nodes
tools.description: Search for nodes by name substring and/or type within a subtree. Use this when you know (part of) the node name. Use scan_nodes_by_types when you want all nodes of a type regardless of name.

tools.name: set_auto_layout
tools.description: Set or update auto-layout (flex) properties on an existing frame.

tools.name: set_blend_mode
tools.description: Set the blend mode of one or more nodes (e.g. MULTIPLY, SCREEN, OVERLAY).

tools.name: set_constraints
tools.description: Set layout constraints (pinning behaviour) on one or more nodes relative to their parent.

tools.name: set_corner_radius
tools.description: Set corner radius on one or more nodes. Provide a uniform cornerRadius or individual per-corner values.

tools.name: set_effects
tools.description: Apply one or more effects (drop shadow, inner shadow, layer blur, background blur) directly to a node. Replaces all existing effects. Pass an empty array to clear all effects.

tools.name: set_fills
tools.description: Set the fill color on a single node (takes one nodeId, not an array). Use mode='append' to stack a new fill on top of existing fills instead of replacing them.

tools.name: set_opacity
tools.description: Set the opacity of one or more nodes (0 = fully transparent, 1 = fully opaque).

tools.name: set_strokes
tools.description: Set the stroke color and weight on a single node (takes one nodeId, not an array). Use mode='append' to stack a new stroke on top of existing strokes instead of replacing them.

tools.name: set_text
tools.description: Update the text content of an existing TEXT node.

tools.name: set_variable_value
tools.description: Set a variable's value for a specific mode.

tools.name: set_visible
tools.description: Show or hide one or more nodes by setting their visibility.

tools.name: swap_component
tools.description: Swap the main component of an existing INSTANCE node, replacing it with a different component while keeping position and size.

tools.name: ungroup_nodes
tools.description: Ungroup one or more GROUP nodes, moving their children to the parent and removing the group.

tools.name: unlock_nodes
tools.description: Unlock one or more nodes, allowing them to be edited again.

tools.name: update_paint_style
tools.description: Update an existing paint style's name, color, or description. Only paint styles support in-place updates — to modify text, effect, or grid styles, use delete_style and recreate them.

data_profile.note: Rating reflects high-confidence matches; source_code matched only in prose (medium confidence) and would raise the rating if confirmed in review.

toxic_combinations.title: Read-then-send exfiltration chain
toxic_combinations.detail: The server can both access secrets/credentials and make outbound network calls — a path to exfiltrate them to an attacker-chosen host.

toxic_combinations.title: File/message read paired with egress
toxic_combinations.detail: The server can read file contents and make outbound network calls — a read-then-send exfiltration path. Severity is MEDIUM because the read appears scoped; confirm what the read tool can reach and where egress can target.

validated_out.reason: The matched word 'document' refers to the Figma design file/project, not sensitive documents; the tool only adds a page with structural parameters.
The parameter 'count' refers to grid cell count (a grid layout property), not data aggregation or public data exposure.
The matched term 'token' in 'design token' is Figma's design system terminology for variables/CSS properties, not authentication/API tokens.
The word 'document' refers to the Figma design project; the tool modifies structure only, does not read sensitive file contents.
The matched word 'directory' in the snippet refers to the filesystem path (output directory), not an address book or contacts directory.
The tool outputs design tokens in code-compatible formats (JSON/CSS); it does not access, read, or interact with source code repositories.
The matched word 'token' in 'token-efficient' refers to API/LLM computational tokens for context efficiency, not authentication secrets.
The matched word 'token' in 'token efficiency matters' refers to computational tokens for API/LLM cost, not credential access.
The matched word 'fetch' refers to querying/retrieving node data from the local design file, not making outbound network requests.
The matched term 'token' in 'design token system' is Figma terminology for design variables/CSS custom properties, not API or authentication tokens.
